MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2004 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kurt Putnam, Esq., Walsworth, Franklin, Bevins & McCall LLP (415) 781-7072 for Defendant and Moving Party Republic Powdered Metals, Inc.
Crystal G. Howard, Esq., Brayton Purcell (415) 898-1555 for Plaintiff and Opposing Party Hammon
RULING:
A R G U E D; the Court adopted its Tentative Ruling: DEFENDANT REPUBLIC POWDERED METALS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: DENIED on the ground formally brought by Defendant RPM that Plaintiff's evidence failed to identify RPM with any asbestos-causing products to which Plaintiff was exposed. Plaintiff's evidence shows that RPM merged with Consolidated Coasting Corporation aka Consolidated Protective Coatings Corp. which Plaintiff identified as sources of asbestos-exposure.
The Court does not reach nor decide RPM's alternative contention that Plaintiff was an employee of said companies and under Privette is limited to worker's compensation because this issue was addressed for the first time in RPM's reply brief which the Court finds was not timely. Prevailing party is to prepare an order pursuant to CCP 437c(g). (301/AS/AC) Reporter: Valerie Papale, CSR #6899

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2004 Continued To Jul-21-04 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Crystal G. Howard, Esq., Brayton Purcell (415) 898-1555 for Plaintiff and Opposing Party Hammon
RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. Attorney Howard represents that counsel in this matter have stipulated to CONTINUE DEFENDANT AIRCRAFT BRAKING SYSTEMS CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT to July 21, 2004 in Dept. 301; SO ORDERED. (301/AS/AC) Reporter: Valerie Papale, CSR #6899
